comments.body: There's no fix/solution. It's a warning that you might be creating a security flaw if you don't know what you're doing.
Maybe this gives some more information: vulncat.fortify.com/en/…

answers.body:
Does your backend code verify the file's extension?

If your backend code has to check and verify the file's extension, then you can scan without any issues.

Example in C# like the following:

string extension = Path.GetExtension(file.FileName);
            
if (extension.ToLower().Trim() != ".xlsx" && extension.ToLower().Trim() != ".xls")
{
throw new Exception("Check the extension!");
}

and JSP FILE:

<input class="textfield width450" 
type="file" name="File_Name" 
maxlength="255" value="" 
accept=".xlsx,.xls">

It should not be an error.

It is a warning against malicious hacking; denial of service by high server load and stealing disk resources. Especially hundreds of huge garbage files could be sent by a robot. The form's max-length attribute is not sufficient.

Repeat the browser restrictions in the backend:

public void setAttachedFile(FormFile formFile) {
   if (formFile.getFileSize() > 255) {
       throw new IllegalArgumentException("File too large");
   }
   ...
}

(Not sure about which exception is adequate.

But first create your own attack, to test the actual behavior.

To throttle the uploads, have a limited number of uploads and slow down, is a technical issue out of scope of a simple answer.

title: Often Misused: File Upload in Java and JSP file
body:
I am getting the "Often Misused: File Upload" on the below lines. Can anyone suggest the fix.

  1. JAVA file:

    **public void setAttachedFile(FormFile formFile) { // File upload error at this line**
       attachedFile = formFile;

       if (attachedFile != null) {
            formData.put("attachedFile", attachedFile);
       } else {
              formData.remove("attachedFile");
        }

      }

  2. JSP file:

    <table width="100%" border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="3">
            <tr>
              <td class="label" width="1%"> </td>
              <td class="label" width="22%">Select File Name</td>
                 <td class="field" width="26%">
             **<input class="textfield width450" type="file" name="File_Name" maxlength="255" value=""> // Getting                  the fortify here**
                 </td>               
               </tr>  
           </table>

Can anyone please suggest the fix/solution
